Build one JSON AST module for a support escalation ladder.

The module must be named demo.support-escalation.

The program receives a collection named tickets. Each ticket has these fields:

- ticket-id: string
- account-id: string
- tier: string
- sentiment-score: number
- open-hours: number
- revenue-impact: number
- outage: boolean
- replies-without-resolution: number
- owner-email: string or missing

Create the main function escalation-report that:

- accepts tickets
- returns a query over tickets as ticket
- keeps only tickets where outage is true or sentiment-score is less than 40 or open-hours is greater than 24
- sorts by ticket.ticket-id ascending
- selects a map row with exactly these keys:
  - ticket-id from ticket.ticket-id
  - account-id from ticket.account-id
  - escalation is "incident" when outage is true and revenue-impact is greater than 10000, "executive-review" when revenue-impact is greater than 50000, "sentiment-save" when sentiment-score is less than 40, otherwise "sla-watch"
  - queue is "war-room" when outage is true, "retention" when sentiment-score is less than 40, otherwise "support-lead"
  - stale is "yes" when replies-without-resolution is greater than 3, otherwise "no"
  - owner is owner-email when present, otherwise "support-pool"

Do not include healthy tickets outside the filter.
Do not invent fields.
